title: ParameterFactory uses HashMap<Type, Func<...>> instead of [SmartEnum<T>] dispatch
evidence: `internal static readonly HashMap<Type, Func<string,string,string,Access,IParameter>> Registry = HashMap((typeof(Point3d), ...), (typeof(Vector3d), ...), ...)` then `Build(Type kind, ...)` does `Registry.Find(key: kind).Match(Some: factory => factory(...), None: () => Fallback(...))`.
violation: Stringly-typed (typeof-keyed) dispatch table contradicts coding-csharp policy: 'Polymorphic — one entrypoint per concern, generic over specific'. Adding a new geometry parameter requires editing the table; missing entries silently fall through to GenericParameter — no compile-time exhaustiveness. coding-csharp paradigm rule: 'Programmatic logic: SmartEnum<T> over strings, bounded vocabularies'.
proposal: Convert to `[SmartEnum<GeometryParameterKind>]` partial class enumerating each (CLR-Type, IParameter-Factory) pair as a typed member. Generated `Switch`/`Map` makes the union exhaustive at compile time; a new geometry type forces a member addition. The factory delegate then becomes a typed projection on the enum value, not a stringly-keyed lookup. Lookup becomes `GeometryParameterKind.From(typeof(TInput)).Map(kind => kind.Build(name, code, info, access)).IfNone(() => Fallback)`.
surface_break: None for Bridge.cs/Component subclasses — Build/BuildIndex signatures preserved

title: ParameterFactory re-implements GH2 InputAdder.Add{Curve|Brep|Mesh|Point|...} dispatch
evidence: Bridge constructs `(IParameter)new CurveParameter(n,c,i,a)` etc. via reflection-keyed table. The GH2 InputAdder/OutputAdder API already provides 50+ typed `AddCurve(string,string,string,Access,Requirement)`, `AddSurface(...)`, `AddBrep` aliased through `AddSurface`, `AddMesh(...)`, `AddPoint(...)`, `AddVector(...)`, `AddBox(...)`, `AddPlane(...)`, `AddLine(...)`, `AddCircle(...)`, `AddArc(...)`, `AddSphere(...)`, `AddInteger`, `AddIndex`, `AddNumber`, `AddNumeric`, `AddGeneric`, etc. — verified by reflection on `Grasshopper2.Components.InputAdder` and `OutputAdder` (see /tmp/gh2_inspect probe).
violation: DEPENDENCY_POLICY: 'Treat dependencies declared in ... Directory.Build.props ... as first-class libraries. Integrate approved external libraries directly; use native APIs end-to-end. Hand-roll functionality already provided by approved dependencies — NEVER.' The `ParameterFactory.Build` registry duplicates exactly the per-CLR-type IParameter construction the GH2 API already owns.
proposal: Eliminate `ParameterFactory.Registry` and `Build` entirely. The polymorphic seam is the SmartEnum's `AddTo(InputAdder, ...)`/`AddTo(OutputAdder, ...)` member function which closes over the typed `InputAdder.Add{X}` call. Equivalently: store a `Func<InputAdder, string, string, string, Access, Requirement, IParameter>` per enum case bound to the typed AddX method group. This collapses the bridge's reflection layer onto GH2-native typed dispatch and eliminates the `(IParameter)new XParameter(...)` allocations.
loc_delta: -23 (delete Registry+Fallback+Build) and replace with SmartEnum projections (~+18) — net -5
surface_break: AnalysisComponent<TInput> calls migrate from `ParameterFactory.Build(typeof(TInput),...)` to the typed adder method on the enum

title: AnalysisComponent<object> erases the geometry constraint
evidence: `public sealed class ExtractPointsComponent : AnalysisComponent<object>` and `BridgeOutput<object, Point3d>(...)` — the generic argument `<object>` admits any CLR type but every Query inside the Outputs Seq pattern-matches on `Curve | Brep | Mesh | SubD | Polyline | Box | ...` (all `GeometryBase`-rooted, plus value-type primitives). Same pattern in ExtractSurfacesComponent.cs:14.
violation: coding-csharp Type-discipline: 'Strongly typed — explicit types everywhere, one canonical shape per concept, zero var.' `<object>` weakens the boundary contract — `Process` line 149 then has to do `(access.GetItem<object>(0, out item), item) switch { (true, TInput input) => ... }` which is a runtime cast at the boundary instead of a typed acceptance.
proposal: Constraint cannot be `<TInput : GeometryBase>` because the analysis layer accepts value-type primitives (Box, BoundingBox, Line, Plane, Sphere, Circle, Arc — all structs, none deriving from GeometryBase). The correct domain shape is a closed [Union] `RhinoGeometry { Native(GeometryBase), Box, BoundingBox, Line, Polyline, Plane, Sphere, Cylinder, Cone, Torus, Circle, Arc }` carried as the canonical input type. AnalysisComponent constraint becomes `where TInput : RhinoGeometry`. Components that accept the universal set declare `AnalysisComponent<RhinoGeometry>`; surface-only components declare `AnalysisComponent<RhinoSurface>` (Brep | Surface | SubD subset). The GenericParameter fallback then disappears because every constraint maps to a concrete IParameter via D-001's SmartEnum.
loc_delta: +45 (Union declaration in core/grasshopper) / 0 in components — net +45 with full type safety
surface_break: ExtractPoints/ExtractSurfaces type signatures change; analysis Query dispatch already does the same union-style switch internally so semantics are unchanged

title: Process performs runtime type-cast at the boundary instead of using IDataAccess.GetItem<TInput>
evidence: `_ = (access.GetItem(index: 0, value: out object? item), item) switch { (true, TInput input) => Outputs.Iter(...), _ => HandleMissingInput(...) };` — this calls the generic `GetItem<object>` then runtime-pattern-matches on `TInput`.
violation: GH2 IDataAccess.GetItem is `public Boolean GetItem<T>(Int32 index, out T value)` — generic-typed. Calling with `<object>` and then casting via switch defeats the GH2 type machinery (which uses TypeAssistant<T> and TypeUnification under the hood to coerce upstream tree leaves into T).
proposal: Once D-003 lands, replace with `(access.GetItem<TInput>(index: 0, value: out TInput input), input) switch { (true, _) => ..., _ => HandleMissingInput(access) };`. The cast becomes the GH2-supplied coercion path; `(true, TInput input)` becomes definitionally true so the switch collapses to `access.GetItem<TInput>(0, out TInput input) switch { true => ..., false => HandleMissingInput(access) };`.

title: BuildIndex re-implements InputAdder.AddIndex
evidence: `internal static IParameter BuildIndex(string name, string code, string info, Access access) => (IParameter)new IntegerParameter(name, code, info, access) { IsIndex = true };` plus `AddIndexSlot(InputAdder inputs, IndexInputSpec spec)` at lines 114-123.
violation: GH2 InputAdder declares `public IntegerParameter AddIndex(String name, String code, String info, Access access, Requirement requirement)` — verified by reflection. The bridge re-creates this exact method by hand and routes through a private `AddIndexSlot` helper.
proposal: Delete `ParameterFactory.BuildIndex` and `AnalysisComponent.AddIndexSlot`. Replace the `IndexInput.Match` arm with `IndexInput.Iter(spec => inputs.AddIndex(spec.Name, spec.Code, spec.Description, Access.Item, spec.Requirement))`. Eliminates one helper and one factory entry.

title: AddMissingInputError is a single-call thin wrapper over IDataAccess.AddError
evidence: `public static Unit AddMissingInputError(this IDataAccess access, string label, string accepted = Defaults.AcceptedGeometry) { ... access.AddError(text: label, details: $"{label} input is required. Connect: {accepted}."); return Unit.Default; }`. Sole call-site: AnalysisComponent.cs:156 `access.AddMissingInputError(label: Input.Name)`.
violation: coding-csharp Surface rules: 'No helper files, no single-caller extracted functions, no one-use file-level declarations.' Single-call extension method that does nothing the GH2 API doesn't already do.
proposal: Inline at the sole call site: `access.AddError(text: Input.Name, details: $"{Input.Name} input is required. Connect: {Defaults.AcceptedGeometry}.");`. Delete `Defaults.AcceptedGeometry` if not reused (it's not).

title: Warn is a single-callsite helper that could inline
evidence: `internal static Unit Warn<TValue>(IDataAccess access, int index, string name, Error error) { access.AddWarning(text: name, details: error.Message); return WriteValues<TValue>(access: access, index: index, values: []); }`. Sole call-site: Bridge.cs:100 (the `Fail` arm of RunOne's Match).
violation: Helper extracted for one caller. coding-csharp Surface rules forbid single-call extracted functions.
proposal: Inline into the `Fail` arm of RunOne: `Fail: (Error error) => { access.AddWarning(text: name, details: error.Message); return WriteValues<TValue>(access, index, []); }`. Or fold into the Match arm with a local lambda. Note: `WriteValues` itself is also a thin wrapper over `access.SetTwig` — see D-009.

title: ResolveScope+ResolveRuntime+RemarkAndFallback split a single boundary adapter into three named helpers
evidence: Three operations form a single boundary adapter: `ResolveScope` (public extension on IDataAccess), `ResolveRuntime` (private Fin<AnalysisRuntime>), `RemarkAndFallback` (private fallback path). All three exist for the single concern: 'extract a runtime from IDataAccess; if missing, warn and fall back to mm-default.'
violation: coding-csharp Private integration: 'private/internal members are nested types, closures, or inline compositions inside the public class/service, not standalone file-level declarations consumed by a single caller.' Three top-level static methods for one concern. Also: `RemarkAndFallback` uses an explicit `throw new InvalidOperationException` (line 85) which is gated by a 'BOUNDARY ADAPTER' comment — but this is reachable only when `Analyze.In(Millimeters).Runtime` fails, which the comment notes is unreachable. The throw is dead code that the analyzer (CSP-rule on throw in domain) flags.
proposal: Collapse to a single expression body: `public static AnalysisRuntime ResolveScope(this IDataAccess access) => Analyze.In(absolute: access.GetTolerance(out double a) ? a : RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance + 0.01, ...).Runtime.IfFail(_ => { access.AddRemark(...); return Analyze.In(Millimeters).Runtime.IfFail(error => throw new InvalidOperationException(error.Message)); });`. Or better: lift the fallback into `Analyze.In(...).Runtime` returning a `Fin<AnalysisRuntime>` and let the domain own the failure rail — ResolveScope returns `Fin<AnalysisRuntime>` and `Process` Binds on it. Eliminates the throw entirely.
surface_break: `ResolveScope` return type changes from `AnalysisRuntime` to `Fin<AnalysisRuntime>` if the failure-rail option is taken — ripple through Process

title: WriteValues hides SetTwig allocation behind a thin wrapper
evidence: `internal static Unit WriteValues<TValue>(IDataAccess access, int index, TValue[] values) { access.SetTwig<TValue>(index: index, values: values, metas: values.Length switch { 0 => [], _ => new MetaData[values.Length] }, nulls: values.Length switch { 0 => [], _ => new bool[values.Length] }); return Unit.Default; }`. Called from RunOne, Warn, BridgeOutput.WriteEmpty.
violation: `metas`/`nulls` arrays are always allocated zeroed — `new MetaData[n]` and `new bool[n]` produce default-initialised arrays. The 0-length switch is a micro-optimisation that conflates two cases. The wrapper exists to centralize the allocation pattern but the pattern is one line of GH2 API plus two `new[]`.
proposal: Either (a) inline at all three call sites, or (b) keep but simplify: `access.SetTwig<TValue>(index, values, new MetaData[values.Length], new bool[values.Length]); return Unit.Default;` — drop the 0-length branch (zero-length array allocation is cheaper than a switch). The 3 call sites become 1-liner GH2 calls plus the RunOne/Match arms get cleaner.

title: PluginBase passes Assembly redundantly — Plugin base auto-derives it
evidence: `public abstract class PluginBase(Guid id, Assembly source, string fallbackName) : Plugin(id: id, nomen: new Nomen(source.GetCustomAttribute<AssemblyTitleAttribute>()?.Title ?? fallbackName, ...), version: source.GetName().Version)`. Reflection on Grasshopper2.Framework.Plugin shows: `public Assembly Assembly { get; }` is auto-implemented by the base class (verified, see /tmp/gh2_inspect probe), so `Plugin` already knows its own assembly without subclasses passing one in.
violation: DEPENDENCY_POLICY: 'NEVER hand-roll functionality already provided by approved dependencies.' The `source` parameter duplicates Plugin.Assembly. RadyabLibrary.cs:13 passes `typeof(RadyabLibrary).Assembly` — the same value Plugin.Assembly resolves to.
proposal: Drop the `Assembly source` constructor parameter. Read attributes via `Assembly.GetCustomAttribute<...>()` lazily inside the override bodies: `public override string Author => Assembly.GetCustomAttribute<AssemblyCompanyAttribute>()?.Company ?? fallbackName;`. The Nomen at Plugin.ctor still needs the title at construction — pass `Assembly.GetCallingAssembly()` or use a default-resolved approach. RadyabLibrary's signature reduces to `: base(id: ..., fallbackName: "Radyab")`.
surface_break: PluginBase ctor signature changes — single subclass (RadyabLibrary), trivial migration

title: 5 Outputs hand-rolled instead of vocabulary-driven
evidence: `Outputs { get; } = Seq<IBridgeOutput<object>>(new BridgeOutput<object, Point3d>(Name: "Edge Midpoints", Code: "EM", Description: "...", Query: Analysis.Query.EdgeMidpoints<object, Point3d>()), new BridgeOutput<object, Point3d>(Name: "Center", ...), new BridgeOutput<object, Point3d>(Name: "Vertices", ...), new BridgeOutput<object, Point3d>(Name: "Bounding Corners", ...), new BridgeOutput<object, Point3d>(Name: "Quadrants", ...))` — 5 hand-rolled records. ExtractSurfacesComponent.cs:15-40 has the parallel structure with 4 Brep outputs + 1 Plane output.
violation: User directive: 'algorithmic logic and variable driven code to reduce the amount of fixed members'. coding-csharp Programmatic principle: 'Bounded vocabularies'. Each component is 5 parallel record literals — adding a new aspect (e.g. 'Centroid by Mass Type') means one more literal, no compile-time discipline. The (Name, Code, Description, Query) tuple is a vocabulary that should be a SmartEnum.
proposal: Define `[SmartEnum<PointAspect>]` partial class with members `EdgeMidpoints`, `Center`, `Vertices`, `BoundingCorners`, `Quadrants` — each member carries the (Name, Code, Description, Query-factory) tuple. Then `Outputs` derives from the enum: `protected override Seq<IBridgeOutput<TInput>> Outputs { get; } = PointAspect.Items.Map(a => a.ToBridgeOutput<TInput>()).ToSeq();` (or via a static `BridgeOutput.From(PointAspect)`). ExtractSurfacesComponent gets `[SmartEnum<SurfaceAspect>]` with `AllSurfaces`, `TopSurface`, `BottomSurface`, `IndexedSurface`, `UVFrame`. The Faces/FaceFrame inputs to the Query already use the `Faces` Union from analysis — the SmartEnum closes over the right Faces case at member declaration. Adding a new aspect requires adding one enum member; the Outputs Seq updates automatically.
loc_delta: +30 SmartEnum decl, -25 in components — net +5 with compile-time vocabulary
surface_break: BridgeOutput record may need a constructor that accepts a vocabulary enum member; minor

title: Surface aspect vocabulary duplicates the analysis Faces Union
evidence: `Outputs` declares 5 outputs, four of which are `Faces.All`/`Faces.Top`/`Faces.Bottom`/`Faces.At` projections; the fifth is `FaceFrame(aspect: new Faces.At())`. The `Faces` [Union] from Analysis.Query.cs:172-177 already enumerates exactly these cases.
violation: coding-csharp 'One canonical type per concept; derive projections, never parallel types.' The boundary layer's surface-aspect vocabulary is a literal projection of the existing `Faces` Union. Currently the projection is implicit (4 of 5 outputs select a `Faces` case); making it explicit removes redundancy.
proposal: Cross-cutting with D-011: SurfaceAspect SmartEnum has members directly bound to `Faces` cases (`AllSurfaces -> Faces.All`, `TopSurface -> Faces.Top`, `BottomSurface -> Faces.Bottom`, `IndexedSurface -> Faces.At`) plus one composite (`UVFrame -> FaceFrame.At`). Output's Query is then `enum.SelectQuery<TInput,TOut>()` projecting the closed-over Faces case to `Analysis.Query.Faces<TInput,Brep>(aspect: faces)` or `Analysis.Query.FaceFrame<TInput,Plane>(aspect: faces)`. The SmartEnum binds enum-case-to-Query-factory once; the component instantiates 5 outputs from one Items projection.

title: RemarkAndFallback emits an unreachable throw at the boundary
evidence: `return Analyze.In(units: Rhino.UnitSystem.Millimeters).Runtime.Match(Succ: static (AnalysisRuntime runtime) => runtime, Fail: static (Error error) => throw new InvalidOperationException(message: error.Message));`. The 'BOUNDARY ADAPTER — Millimetres validates by construction; throw is unreachable' comment acknowledges the throw is dead but required by the synchronous return type.
violation: coding-csharp 'NEVER use exception-style control flow in domain logic; use typed error rails.' The boundary tier is the right place to commit the rail — but the right shape is 'return Fin<AnalysisRuntime>' and let Process Bind on it, not throw inside a Match arm. The CS-analyzer's CSP rule on throw inside domain pipelines flags this even with the BOUNDARY ADAPTER marker.
proposal: Lift the boundary commitment up: `ResolveScope` returns `Fin<AnalysisRuntime>` (or `Validation<Error,AnalysisRuntime>`); Process Binds: `(from rt in access.ResolveScope() ... ).Match(Succ: ..., Fail: error => { access.AddError("Tolerance", error.Message); ... })`. Eliminates the throw and unifies the failure rail with RunOne's existing failure handling.
surface_break: ResolveScope return type changes — single caller (Process), trivial

title: AddIndexSlot is a 9-line single-call helper
evidence: `private static Unit AddIndexSlot(InputAdder inputs, IndexInputSpec spec) { inputs.Add(parameter: ParameterFactory.BuildIndex(name: spec.Name, code: spec.Code, info: spec.Description, access: Access.Item), requirement: spec.Requirement); return Unit.Default; }`. Sole call: AnalysisComponent.cs:111 inside the IndexInput.Match Some arm.
violation: Single-caller helper.
proposal: With D-005 in place, this collapses to `IndexInput.Iter(spec => inputs.AddIndex(spec.Name, spec.Code, spec.Description, Access.Item, spec.Requirement))` inline in AddInputs.

title: HandleMissingInput is a single-call private helper
evidence: `private Unit HandleMissingInput(IDataAccess access) { _ = access.AddMissingInputError(label: Input.Name); return Outputs.Iter((int slot, IBridgeOutput<TInput> output) => output.WriteEmpty(access: access, index: slot)); }`. Sole call: line 152 inside Process's switch.
violation: Single-call helper. Combined with D-006 (AddMissingInputError is itself a single-call wrapper), this is a chain of two thin layers.
proposal: Inline at the switch arm: `_ => { access.AddError(text: Input.Name, details: $"{Input.Name} input is required. Connect: {Defaults.AcceptedGeometry}."); return Outputs.Iter((slot, output) => output.WriteEmpty(access, slot)); }`. With D-003 (TInput constrained), this becomes the only non-success branch and reduces to a 2-line lambda.

title: Index-input integration uses nested Match-with-record-update — algebraic but verbose
evidence: `AnalysisRuntime scoped = IndexInput.Match(Some: (IndexInputSpec spec) => scope with { Index = IndexHint.Create(value: (access.GetItem(index: 1, value: out int candidate), candidate) switch { (true, int value) => value, _ => spec.Default, }).Match(Succ: static (IndexHint hint) => Some(hint), Fail: static (Error _) => Option<IndexHint>.None) }, None: () => scope);` — 12 lines, two Match expressions, three switch arms.
violation: coding-csharp Programmatic — composition is correct but expression density obscures intent. The Index extraction is: 'optional spec; if present, optionally read a candidate from access[1], else default; either way clamp to IndexHint.Create' — three Options collapsed.
proposal: Extract as a private static method on AnalysisRuntime or a pipeline operator: `scope.WithIndex(IndexInput, access)` where the extension lives next to AnalysisRuntime in core/Domain (already has `Analyze.Scope.WithIndex(int)` per Analyze.cs:53). The current `scope with { Index = ... }` re-implements WithIndex inline. Reuse: `AnalysisRuntime scoped = IndexInput.Match(Some: spec => Analyze.In(scope.Context).WithIndex(...).Runtime.IfFail(_ => scope), None: () => scope);` — though this re-invokes Analyze.In. A cleaner shape: define `AnalysisRuntime.WithIndex(IDataAccess, IndexInputSpec)` that returns `AnalysisRuntime` directly (mirrors the existing Scope.WithIndex). Bridge layer then delegates to it.
surface_break: Adds public WithIndex method on AnalysisRuntime in Core.Domain — outside Agent D's scope but flagged here

title: AddInputs/AddOutputs both repeat the same factory.Build pattern
evidence: `AddInputs` builds the input parameter via `ParameterFactory.Build(typeof(TInput), Input.Name, Input.Code, Input.Description, Input.Access)` then conditionally adds the index slot. `AddOutputs` iterates `Outputs.Iter((output) => outputs.Add(parameter: ParameterFactory.Build(kind: output.ValueType, name: output.Name, ...)))`. Both call sites have the same shape.
violation: Duplication is inherent to the input/output asymmetry in GH2. Not a hard violation but flagged because once D-002 lands (typed AddX from InputAdder/OutputAdder), the duplication moves into the SmartEnum's adder member, where it's localised to one declaration.
proposal: Subsumed by D-002. SmartEnum.AddTo(InputAdder|OutputAdder, name, code, info, access, requirement?) member centralises the dispatch, called once from each override.
loc_delta: +0 (no change in this file beyond D-002)

title: Defaults.AcceptedGeometry is a single-use constant
evidence: `internal static class Defaults { internal const string AcceptedGeometry = "Curve, Brep, Mesh, SubD, Surface, Box, BoundingBox, Line, Polyline, Sphere, Cylinder, Cone, Torus, Plane"; }`. Sole reference: AddMissingInputError default parameter (line 56).
violation: coding-csharp 'Forbidden: helper/utility files... single-caller... constant spam.' This single string only feeds one default parameter that itself is consumed by one call site (AnalysisComponent line 156).
proposal: Inline at the AddError call site (after D-006). Better: drop entirely — once D-003 (Union RhinoGeometry) lands, the accepted geometry list is derivable from the Union's type parameters at compile time. The error message becomes: `$"{Input.Name} input is required. Connect: {string.Join(", ", typeof(TInput).GetSubtypeNames())}"` — automatic and exhaustive.

title: Component constructors are boilerplate Nomen + IReader
evidence: Both components follow `public X() : base(nomen: new Nomen("Display Name", "Description", "Radyab", "Extraction")) { } public X(IReader reader) : base(reader: reader) { }` — 4 lines of identical structure with only the Nomen tuple differing.
violation: User directive: variable-driven code over fixed members. Each component repeats the IReader ctor (required by GH2 deserialisation) and the Nomen ctor with a distinct 4-tuple of strings.
proposal: Move Nomen up into the SmartEnum metadata — the SmartEnum has a `Nomen` per component-class, and AnalysisComponent's primary ctor reads it: `protected AnalysisComponent() : base(nomen: ComponentNomen.For(this.GetType()).Value) { }`. Eliminates the parameterless ctor body. The IReader ctor remains (required) but reduces to one liner.
surface_break: Requires registry lookup keyed by component Type — alternatively, decorate component classes with `[Nomen("Extract Points", ..., "Radyab", "Extraction")]` attribute and read in PluginBase or the GH2 Plugin discovery path

title: InputSpec.Generic and IndexInputSpec.Standard are 'enums of one' single-use defaults
evidence: `public static InputSpec Generic => new(Name: "Geometry", Code: "G", Description: "Geometry to analyse.", Access: Access.Item, Requirement: Requirement.MustExist);` (line 21-22) and `public static IndexInputSpec Standard => new(Name: "Index", Code: "I", Description: "Zero-based selector; clamped to the available range.", Default: 0, Requirement: Requirement.MayBeMissing);` (line 31-34). Both are single-default-instance value containers.
violation: Two-of-one `InputSpec` (only `Generic` is ever materialised in the audited subclasses; ExtractPointsComponent uses default, ExtractSurfacesComponent overrides only IndexInput). The record's flexibility-for-future-needs is unused.
proposal: If only one shape is ever used, embed the constants in the AnalysisComponent default `Input` property and delete `InputSpec.Generic`. Same for `IndexInputSpec.Standard`. If multiple subclasses do specialise InputSpec (other apps?), keep the records but expose factory methods on AnalysisComponent itself rather than on the spec records (Surface API should expose vocabularies, not the data records that carry them).
loc_delta: -7 if collapsed; 0 if multiple specialisations exist (verify across full plugin set)
surface_break: InputSpec.Generic / IndexInputSpec.Standard removal — verify no other plugins consume them

title: AssemblyInfo.cs reduces to two attribute lines
evidence: 6 lines total: 2 usings, 3 assembly attributes (PlugInDescription Organization, PlugInDescription Icon, Guid), 1 blank.
violation: None — this is canonical Rhino assembly metadata. Flagged for completeness.
proposal: None. Acceptable as is. Confirm the Guid `694d39bb-086a-400d-9a31-ef71e0d7b8fe` matches Rhino plug-in registration; it differs from RadyabLibrary's GH2 plugin Guid `6fbf37d9-7fb6-49d1-a7c2-668f4eb36db1` — verify intentional (Rhino plug-in vs Grasshopper plug-in are distinct registrations).

title: RadyabPlugin OnLoad always returns Success
evidence: `public sealed class RadyabPlugin : PlugIn { protected override LoadReturnCode OnLoad(ref string errorMessage) => LoadReturnCode.Success; }` — no actual load logic.
violation: None — the comment explains this exists solely to satisfy `HostUtils.CreatePlugIn` for the .rhp loader. Flagged for completeness.
proposal: None. Minimal Rhino plug-in shell; cannot be reduced further. The Guid in AssemblyInfo couples this to the .rhp loader.

title: FaceFrame output uses a different value type (Plane) breaking output uniformity
evidence: 4 of 5 surface outputs return `Brep`; the 5th (`UV Frame`) returns `Plane`. The hand-rolled `BridgeOutput<object, Plane>(...)` switches the value-type generic mid-Seq, which works but breaks any 'all outputs of this component share a value type' invariant.
violation: Not a hard rule violation, but it confirms D-011's SmartEnum proposal is the right shape: a SurfaceAspect enum where each member's TOut is part of the case (Brep for AllSurfaces/Top/Bottom/Indexed; Plane for UVFrame) eliminates the visual asymmetry and makes the type heterogeneity explicit at the vocabulary level.
proposal: Subsumed by D-011/D-012. The SmartEnum case for `UVFrame` carries `TOut = Plane` while the others carry `TOut = Brep`; the common contract is `IBridgeOutput<TInput>` (already polymorphic on TValue via `ValueType` projection).

title: ParameterFactory only covers 12 of 50+ standard parameters
evidence: Registry maps Point3d, Vector3d, Curve, Surface, Brep, Mesh, Box, Plane, Line, Circle, Arc, Sphere — 12 entries. GH2 Standard Parameter set (verified via reflection): 50+ types including Angle, Boolean, Cage, Colour, Complex, ContinuousDistribution, CurveLocus, DateTime, Deform, DiscreteDistribution, Field, Function, Gradient, Graph, Guid, Integer, Interval, Language, MeshFacet, MetaData, MetaName, Number, Numeric, Path, Point2, PointN, Polyline, Random, Rectangle, Region, Site, SubD (via SurfaceParameter), SurfaceLocus, Text, TextDot, TimeSpan, Transform, Triangle, Tube, Tuple. Anything not in the Registry falls through to `GenericParameter` — silent type erasure at the GH2 boundary.
violation: The fallback path silently weakens the GH2 type contract. A `BridgeOutput<TInput, TimeSpan>` would silently bind to `GenericParameter` instead of `TimeSpanParameter`, and downstream consumers lose the typed coercion path. coding-csharp 'one canonical shape per concept' fails when the concept (a typed parameter) silently falls back to the universal one.
proposal: Subsumed by D-001/D-002. The SmartEnum is closed; adding a new TOut to a BridgeOutput forces a corresponding enum member or a compile-time cast failure. No silent GenericParameter fallback. If a fallback is genuinely required (open-ended TOut from analysis library extensions), make it an explicit Option<GeometryParameterKind> at the BridgeOutput-construction site.
loc_delta: +0 in the Registry (subsumed); risk reduction in the boundary contract
